questions.title: Packaging waste
questions.question: Customers complain about packaging waste. What should your company do?
questions.info: Packaging impact depends on both material type and total waste volume. Plastic-heavy packaging tends to create higher end-of-life impact, recyclable materials improve recovery, and minimal packaging avoids material use at the source.
questions.best_reason: Minimal packaging is best because avoiding material use usually beats only changing the material.

questions.title: Cloud provider choice
questions.question: You need to choose a cloud provider for a new application.

choices.text: Choose a provider that uses renewable energy
Choose a local provider to keep data in your country

questions.info: Cloud impact depends on electricity mix, data-center efficiency, cooling, and utilization. A cheap provider is not necessarily low-carbon, while renewable-powered infrastructure can significantly reduce footprint.
questions.best_reason: A renewable-energy provider has the strongest environmental score in this scenario.

questions.title: Factory location
questions.question: Your company needs to choose where to produce its products. You must decide between different factory locations.

choices.text: Use a factory in Eastern Europe

questions.info: Factory location affects both production electricity and logistics distance. Production in a lower-carbon electricity system and closer market can reduce emissions compared with distant high-carbon manufacturing.
questions.best_reason: France is treated as the lowest-carbon location among the options in this model.

questions.title: AI delivery planning
questions.question: You want to use AI to improve delivery planning. You must decide where to run the system.

choices.text: Run the AI locally on your own servers

questions.info: AI systems can require significant compute. Impact depends on model size, usage volume, hardware utilization, and whether the workload is optimized locally or run through general cloud services.
questions.best_reason: Running optimized AI locally scores best here because it reduces overuse of high-impact cloud compute.

questions.title: Goods transport
questions.question: You need to organize how goods are transported between warehouses.
questions.info: Freight emissions vary strongly by mode. Rail generally emits much less per tonne-kilometre than road freight, while trucks are more flexible but more carbon-intensive.
questions.best_reason: Using trains when possible gives the strongest environmental improvement.

questions.title: Data center cooling strategy
questions.question: Your data center consumes a lot of energy and produces significant heat. Which cooling strategy should you choose?

choices.text: Liquid cooling system with waste heat recovery for the city

questions.info: Data-center cooling can be a major energy driver. Liquid cooling can improve efficiency, and recovering waste heat creates an additional circular benefit by reusing heat that would otherwise be lost.
questions.best_reason: Liquid cooling with waste-heat recovery gives the highest environmental score.

questions.title: IT infrastructure expansion
questions.question: You need to expand your IT infrastructure.
questions.info: Infrastructure expansion has both embodied and operational impact. Building a private data center can create large construction and utilization impacts, while shared infrastructure may improve hardware utilization.
questions.best_reason: The smaller local provider is slightly positive in this model, assuming efficient/local operation.

questions.title: Employee work policy
questions.question: You need to define the company work policy.
questions.info: Work policy affects commuting and office energy. Remote work can reduce commuting emissions, but home-energy rebound effects should still be considered.
questions.best_reason: Fully remote work scores best when commuting and office-space use are actually reduced.

questions.title: Office hardware replacement
questions.question: You need to replace office hardware.
questions.info: Digital device footprint is dominated by manufacturing. Extending device life and buying refurbished equipment avoids much of the impact from producing new hardware.
questions.best_reason: Refurbished devices score best because they avoid most new manufacturing impact.

questions.title: Energy supplier
questions.question: Your company must choose an energy supplier.
questions.info: Energy supplier choice changes electricity carbon intensity. Renewable electricity is the strongest option, mixed energy is neutral, and cheap unspecified energy may rely on higher-carbon sources.
questions.best_reason: Renewable energy has the best environmental score.

questions.title: Cybersecurity investment
questions.question: You need to decide how much to invest in cybersecurity.
questions.info: Cybersecurity has a small operational footprint, but weak security can create incidents with financial and operational damage. Advanced security adds some compute/storage overhead in this model.
questions.best_reason: Minimal and standard security are tied environmentally; A is marked best only by environmental score, though it increases cyberattack risk.

questions.title: Cyberattack response
questions.question: Your company is hit by a cyberattack that disrupts operations.
questions.info: Incident response can require extra recovery activity and infrastructure use. Slow restoration has little direct environmental effect but can hurt the business financially.
questions.best_reason: Slow restoration has the best direct environmental score, although it may not be the best business decision.

questions.title: Historical data storage
questions.question: Your servers are almost full because your company keeps all historical data.

choices.text: Buy more storage and keep everything

questions.info: Stored data has an ongoing footprint because storage infrastructure consumes energy and hardware. Deleting unused data avoids storage demand; cold archive is better than keeping everything online.
questions.best_reason: Deleting old unused data has the highest environmental score.

questions.title: Supplier choice
questions.question: A supplier is very cheap but not environmentally friendly.

choices.text: Work with the supplier to improve

questions.info: Supplier choice affects upstream production footprint. A cheap but polluting supplier shifts environmental impact into the supply chain; sustainable procurement reduces that footprint.
questions.best_reason: Choosing the sustainable supplier gives the highest environmental score.

questions.title: New office location
questions.question: You need to open a new office for a growing team.

choices.text: Open the office in the city center, with easy access but higher rent
Open the office in a suburban area, cheaper but requiring more commuting

questions.info: Office location affects commuting. City-center locations often support public transport and shorter trips, while suburban locations can increase car dependence.
questions.best_reason: The city-center office is better because it supports lower-carbon commuting.

questions.title: Marketing campaign
questions.question: The marketing team wants to launch a promotional campaign.

choices.text: Use a limited eco-designed print campaign combined with digital communication

questions.info: Marketing campaigns have material and distribution impacts. Mass printed flyers create paper, ink, and logistics footprint; digital communication avoids most physical material use.
questions.best_reason: A limited eco-designed print campaign combined with digital communication gives the best score.

questions.title: Devices left on at night
questions.question: Many company devices remain on during the night.

choices.text: Ask employees to turn off devices

questions.info: Idle devices waste electricity outside working hours. Reminders help, but automatic shutdown is more reliable because it reduces dependency on individual behavior.
questions.best_reason: Automatic shutdown systems provide the strongest reliable reduction.

questions.title: Printing policy
questions.question: Employees print too many documents.

choices.text: Set double-sided black-and-white printing by default
Require digital documents for internal meetings

questions.info: Printing uses paper, ink, hardware, and energy. Duplex black-and-white printing reduces material use, while digital internal documents avoid most printing entirely.
questions.best_reason: Digital documents for internal meetings best avoid printing impact.

questions.title: Software hosting architecture
questions.question: You need to choose the software hosting architecture.
questions.info: Software architecture affects server utilization and operational overhead. Microservices can add network and orchestration overhead; serverless can reduce idle capacity when well designed.
questions.best_reason: Serverless architecture scores best environmentally in this model.

questions.title: Data backup strategy
questions.question: You need to choose a data backup strategy.
questions.info: Backup strategy balances resilience with storage and transfer footprint. Continuous backups duplicate more data, while weekly backups reduce footprint but increase data-loss risk.
questions.best_reason: Weekly backups use the least backup storage and transfer in the environmental model.

questions.title: Office heating costs
questions.question: Energy prices are rising, and heating your offices is becoming very expensive.

choices.text: Maintain heating and absorb the extra cost
Reduce heating and risk employee dissatisfaction
Reduce office usage and rely more on remote work
Invest in building improvements such as insulation and smart systems

questions.info: Heating strategy affects building energy demand. Lower heating and reduced office use save energy; insulation and smart systems can create durable savings but require investment.
questions.best_reason: Reducing office usage and relying more on remote work ties environmentally with building improvements but costs less in this game.

questions.title: Cycling support
questions.question: Employees ask for better cycling support.

choices.text: Do nothing because few employees currently cycle

questions.info: Cycling support can shift commutes toward near-zero direct-emission transport. Doing nothing preserves existing commuting patterns.
questions.best_reason: A bike bonus can produce a strong positive commuting modal shift.

questions.title: Commuting reimbursement policy
questions.question: The company wants to update its commuting reimbursement policy.

choices.text: Increase reimbursement for employees using public transport
Increase fuel reimbursement for employees driving alone
Offer the same fixed transport bonus to everyone

questions.info: Reimbursement policy changes employee commuting incentives. Supporting public transport lowers average commute emissions, while fuel reimbursement can encourage solo driving.
questions.best_reason: Public-transport reimbursement gives the best environmental outcome.

questions.title: Product returns
questions.question: Customers are returning more products, increasing transport and waste.

choices.text: Improve product quality to reduce returns

questions.info: Product returns create reverse logistics, extra handling, and sometimes waste. Preventing returns at the source through better quality is usually stronger than only discouraging returns later.
questions.best_reason: Improving product quality prevents returns at the source and has the highest environmental score.

questions.title: Video conferencing policy
questions.question: You need to define the company video conferencing policy.
questions.info: Video meetings use more data transfer and device energy than audio-only meetings. Using video only when needed is balanced; audio-by-default minimizes data transfer.
questions.best_reason: Audio-only by default has the best environmental score.

questions.title: Investor profitability pressure
questions.question: Investors demand higher profitability this quarter.
questions.info: Profitability pressure can affect long-term sustainability investments. Cutting sustainability may improve short-term money but lock in high-impact operations, while efficiency can help both dimensions.
questions.best_reason: Improving efficiency gives a strong environmental benefit while also improving money.

questions.title: Software update policy
questions.question: You need to decide how frequently systems are updated.

choices.text: Frequent updates, always using the latest versions

questions.info: Software update policy has a trade-off: very frequent updates create testing and deployment churn, while rare updates can preserve inefficient or insecure systems. A balanced cycle avoids both extremes.
questions.best_reason: A balanced update cycle is the best environmental choice here.

questions.title: Logging and monitoring strategy
questions.question: Your systems generate large amounts of logs. You need to choose a logging and monitoring strategy.

choices.text: Keep logs for a limited time
Use intelligent log filtering and compression

questions.info: Logs create storage and processing demand. Keeping all logs indefinitely grows footprint, while filtering and compression reduce data at the source.
questions.best_reason: Intelligent filtering and compression best reduce log volume.

questions.title: Device lifecycle policy
questions.question: Employees request frequent device upgrades. You need to define a device lifecycle policy.
questions.info: Device lifecycle matters because manufacturing dominates digital hardware footprint. Longer lifetimes reduce annualized impact and avoid unnecessary new devices.
questions.best_reason: Extending lifecycle with maintenance is the strongest environmental choice.

questions.title: API design strategy
questions.question: Your services expose APIs to partners. You need to define an API design strategy.

choices.text: No optimization with large data payloads

questions.info: API design affects network payload size and compute processing. Large payloads waste bandwidth and server work; lightweight APIs reduce transfer and processing needs.
questions.best_reason: Highly optimized lightweight APIs give the best environmental score.

questions.title: Testing environment management
questions.question: You need to manage environments for development and testing.

choices.text: Scheduled environments with on and off periods

questions.info: Development and testing environments can waste large amounts of idle compute. Scheduling helps, but on-demand environments minimize unused server hours.
questions.best_reason: On-demand environments minimize idle compute and score best.

questions.title: Office cooling and heating
questions.question: Your office energy consumption is rising. How should you improve cooling and heating?
questions.info: Office cooling and heating depend on building demand and active energy use. Insulation reduces demand, while smart controls optimize usage over time.
questions.best_reason: Smart climate control systems score best in this model.

questions.title: Network traffic optimization
questions.question: Your applications generate large amounts of unnecessary data traffic. What should you do?

choices.text: Redesign the system to minimize data transfer

questions.info: Unnecessary data traffic wastes network, server, and client resources. Compression and caching help, while redesigning the system can reduce transfer at the source.
questions.best_reason: Redesigning to minimize data transfer has the strongest environmental score.

questions.title: Bring your own device policy
questions.question: Employees want to use personal devices for work. You need to define a BYOD policy.

choices.text: Allow employees to bring their own devices

questions.info: BYOD can reduce duplicate devices, but unmanaged personal-device use creates security and governance issues. A controlled hybrid policy balances device reduction and manageability.
questions.best_reason: A controlled hybrid policy is the best balance for environmental impact.

questions.title: AI usage governance
questions.question: Employees are increasingly using external AI tools for work tasks. How should usage be governed?
questions.info: AI governance controls compute demand and data risk. Unrestricted external AI can lead to uncontrolled use; banning minimizes AI compute but may reduce productivity; approved internal tools depend on deployment.
questions.best_reason: Banning external AI has the highest environmental score, though it may not be best for productivity.

questions.title: Green software engineering training
questions.question: Your developers are not trained in energy-efficient coding practices. What training policy should you adopt?
questions.info: Green software training can improve long-term energy efficiency by changing how software is designed and operated. Optional training helps; mandatory training creates systematic change.
questions.best_reason: Mandatory training creates the strongest long-term improvement.

questions.title: Third-party API usage
questions.question: Your system depends heavily on external APIs. You need to define an API dependency strategy.

choices.text: Replace critical APIs with internal solutions

questions.info: External APIs can increase data movement, dependency overhead, and duplicated processing. Limiting calls helps; internalizing critical APIs can reduce repeated calls but may add infrastructure.
questions.best_reason: Replacing critical APIs with internal solutions has the highest environmental score in this model.

questions.title: Product replacement frequency
questions.question: Your products are replaced frequently, generating waste and increasing production costs. What should your company do?

choices.text: Offer a repair or refurbishment service

questions.info: Frequent product replacement creates waste and manufacturing impact. Longer life, repairable design, and repair/refurbishment reduce demand for new production.
questions.best_reason: Designing products for repair ties environmentally with repair services, but avoids extra service cost in this game.

questions.title: Technical debt management
questions.question: Your systems are becoming harder to maintain, causing inefficiencies and increasing energy usage. How should you manage technical debt?

choices.text: Ignore technical debt and continue operations

questions.info: Technical debt can make systems inefficient and hard to maintain. Refactoring and modernization can reduce wasted compute, storage, and operational inefficiency.
questions.best_reason: Full modernization has the strongest environmental improvement in this model.

outcomes.text: A cyberattack targets your company, but your security systems detect it early since you invested in good cybersecurity.
A cyberattack hits your company and manages to disrupt several systems before being stopped.. No major data is lost, but the incident slows down operations and you loose some money.
A cyberattack spreads across your systems without being detected in time since you didn’t invest in cybersecurity. The company struggles to recover, leading financial damage
Customers raise concerns about packaging waste, but since you already use minimal packaging, your company is praised for its efforts and avoids negative impact.
Customers complain about packaging waste. While you switched to recyclable materials, some issues remain visible. The company faces moderate criticism.
Customers strongly criticize your packaging choices, especially due to heavy use of plastic. Complaints spread and customer satisfaction drops, leading to lost revenue.

A journalist investigation reveals your company’s environmental practices, but since you invested in sustainable solutions and made responsible choices, the report highlights your efforts. The company’s image remains strong and trust is maintained.

A media report exposes some environmental weaknesses in your operations. While you followed some sustainable practices, there are still issues that attract criticism. The company’s reputation is slightly affected.

A major media scandal breaks out after your company is exposed for poor environmental practices, especially due to earlier decisions like cheap suppliers or high-emission choices. Public trust drops sharply, customers react negatively.

An internal error or technical failure leads to the loss of important company data. Your continuous backup systems allow for a fast and complete restoration of all lost data. The incident has no lasting consequences.

An internal error or technical failure leads to the loss of important company data. Only part of the data can be recovered. Some information is permanently lost, causing minor disruptions and additional work.

An internal error or technical failure leads to the loss of important company data. Due to poor backup practices, critical data is lost permanently. This affects operations, decision-making, and customer trust, leading to significant losses.

metadata.best_choice_rule: Best choice is the highest environmental score from the data notes. If tied, the option with better budget impact was selected.
metadata.info_source_summary: Info fields are based on the uploaded Data scores PDF methodology: Resilio DB for digital/IT proxies and ADEME/Impact CO2/circular-economy logic for non-digital proxies.
